newsSinceJun6: June 8: Digital Realty launched its Malaysia platform (~32 MW planned), extending APAC footprint - incremental, not thesis-changing for a multi-GW global portfolio. At Nareit REITweek, Stifel reiterated Buy with a $235 target citing AI demand positioning. No earnings, M&A, or new hyperscale lease announcements since June 6; +1.72% day move is ordinary drift.

sources.label: GlobeNewswire - Digital Realty Launches Malaysia Operations (Jun 8, 2026)
Investing.com - Stifel reiterates DLR Buy, $235 PT on AI demand (Nareit REITweek)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already moved DLR up on deployment-capacity relevance; since then the only news is a modest 32 MW Malaysia launch and a sell-side Buy reiteration - confirmation, not new bottleneck evidence, so hold at rank 47 with small positive drift.
verdicts.bull: Record hyperscale leasing backlog (200 MW Charlotte deal), record-low vacancy, and now an APAC growth leg in Malaysia give DLR durable pricing power as AI deployment demand outstrips powered-shell supply.
verdicts.neutral: A $66B, heavily covered REIT up ~19.5% YTD with multiple recent price-target hikes offers limited hidden alpha; the Malaysia 32 MW addition is rounding error versus the global pipeline.
verdicts.bear: Crowded ownership plus rate sensitivity leave the stock exposed if AI leasing momentum or power availability slips, and rising sell-side enthusiasm (Stifel $235, Citizens $250) signals the rerating is well along.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: New hyperscale mega-lease signings (e.g., Charlotte Building 2) or upward 2026 guidance revision at Q2 results.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Rate-driven REIT multiple compression or a pause in hyperscaler capex that stalls leasing velocity after the YTD rerating.
